items.question: Inauguration Day is observed every four years when it falls on which date?
items.correctAnswer: January 20 (or January 21 when the 20th is Sunday)
items.explanation: The 20th Amendment sets January 20 as the start of the new presidential term, with a Sunday exception.

items.question: Independence Day commemorates the adoption of the Declaration of Independence in which year?
items.explanation: July Fourth marks the 1776 declaration by the Continental Congress.

items.question: Juneteenth National Independence Day marks the end of slavery in which state in 1865, later becoming a federal holiday?
items.explanation: Federal troops brought news of emancipation to Galveston, Texas, in June 1865, long celebrated as Juneteenth.

items.question: Memorial Day in May chiefly honors whom?
items.correctAnswer: members of the U.S. military who died in service
items.explanation: Memorial Day focuses on those who died serving the country, distinct from Veterans Day.

items.question: Veterans Day on November 11 primarily recognizes whom?
items.correctAnswer: U.S. veterans who served in the armed forces
items.explanation: Veterans Day honors all who served, while Memorial Day focuses on those who died in service.

items.question: Which federal holiday in January honors the civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr.?
items.explanation: The holiday falls on the third Monday in January and recognizes Dr. King's legacy.
